briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for July 21, 2026. Today, we primarily covered news regarding the deepening global climate crisis, various domestic and international adaptation and carbon neutrality efforts to combat it, and innovative changes in the renewable energy sector.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Global Impacts
sections.content: Recently, Europe has seen intensifying damage from heatwaves, with regions known as climate-advanced countries showing vulnerability to the heat [3, 16, 153]. Furthermore, global oxygen depletion is rapidly progressing in oceans and rivers [5], and the severity of extreme weather events is escalating, with Arctic ice in the Northern Hemisphere's winter decreasing by 5.8% in a year [19]. Direct impacts of climate change are appearing worldwide, such as a fourfold increase in the likelihood of snow drought in the Western U.S. [138] and sudden floods causing casualties in Afghanistan [155]. Amidst this situation, political resistance to climate action continues, with former U.S. President Trump labeling climate change a 'hoax' and ordering an investigation into the court's 'Scientific Evidence Guidelines' [2, 7, 139], adding to concerns.

sections.title: 🌱 Climate Adaptation Efforts and Proactive Responses in Domestic Agriculture
sections.content: Despite the climate crisis, various regions and companies are strengthening proactive adaptation efforts. Boeun-gun is accelerating the establishment of a safe agricultural product production system [4, 9, 14, 28], investing 400 million won in agricultural support projects for climate crisis response to address recurring emergencies in fruit farms due to cold damage and heatwaves [16, 18, 20, 31, 34]. Dongcheonan Nonghyup is exploring crop cultivation suitable for new climate environments by providing specialized training for apple watermelon and apple mango [1], and Anseong-si is also supporting farmers through sesame cultivation technology consulting [17]. In the corporate sector, Lotte E&C is integrating climate risk management as a core element of its operations, for example, by publishing its '2026 ESG Report' and enhancing its financial impact analysis of climate change and natural capital [10, 12, 15, 23, 26, 32, 33, 35, 36].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: At the national level, a blueprint for carbon neutrality and a circular economy transition is being established through the public hearing for the revised 5th National Environmental Comprehensive Plan [40, 43, 50, 60]. Local governments are also actively spreading a culture of carbon neutrality. Geoje-si emphasizes the importance of the climate crisis and carbon neutrality through 'visiting environmental education' [22, 54], and Hwasun-gun operates the 'Carbon Moa Tantane' platform to encourage participation in daily carbon neutrality efforts [39, 48, 53, 65]. Furthermore, the industrial sector is also achieving concrete results in reducing carbon emissions, with Asia Cement obtaining low-carbon product certification for 19 types of ready-mixed concrete using cement with the industry's lowest carbon emissions [64]. These efforts are expected to have a positive effect by lowering the threshold for Korean companies to achieve their goals as the 'Global Essential Carbon Neutrality Certification' process becomes easier [38, 51].

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Innovation and New Power Market Paradigm
sections.content: In the renewable energy sector, innovative policy changes and technological advancements are prominent. Starting in August, the government will launch a pilot project for issuing and trading self-consumption renewable energy certificates ('REGO') [70, 77, 92, 97, 103, 107, 108, 110, 117, 118, 132], opening a path for profit generation beyond electricity bill savings through residential solar power systems [69, 79, 80, 85, 87, 89, 111, 119]. Gyeonggi-do is promoting the issuance of renewable energy certificates to allow household solar power generation to participate in RE100 [71, 84, 99, 113, 114, 115], and is expanding renewable energy supply in water source regulation areas to increase resident income and broaden practical benefits [67, 68, 83, 88, 91, 93, 100, 102, 116, 125, 126, 127, 128, 129]. Additionally, efforts are being accelerated to secure diverse renewable energy sources, such as expanding the use of renewable energy through the rationalization of regulations for livestock manure solid fuel [86, 90, 94, 105, 123, 124]. As the era of 100GW renewable energy officially begins, securing the stability of the power grid is emerging as an important challenge [72, 81, 95], but efforts are also underway to find solutions, such as the operation of a 92MWh BESS (Battery Energy Storage System) in Jeju to address output control issues [98].

briefing.closing: As you've seen in today's briefing, the climate crisis impacts every aspect of our daily lives. However, at the same time, innovative policies, technologies, and active participation from civil society are also expanding to confront this crisis. Moving forward, based on scientific facts about climate change, we will continue to urge practical actions for a sustainable future and meticulously report on that process. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.phenomenon: In Afghanistan's eastern Nuristan province, sudden flash floods on Tuesday resulted in at least 23 deaths, 80 injuries, and over 100 missing persons. These floods destroyed homes and caused severe financial and human losses.
summary.outlook: Rescue crews are searching for survivors and bodies amidst the rubble of destroyed houses, with concerns for additional casualties.
